820	  - `scripts/test/probe-us036-refresh.ts` (new — backend end-to-end probe)
821	  - `scripts/test/metrics-refresh.ts`, `scripts/test/apply-to-listing.ts`, `scripts/test/get-listing-detail.ts`, `scripts/test/submit-video.ts` — local `signMarketifyJwt` helpers updated to mint the new claim shape (caught by code-reviewer as the only critical finding)
822	- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ (the migration is CREATE OR REPLACE on an existing function — no new advisor findings) / mobile-mcp ⚠ backend-path VERIFIED via probe; pull-to-refresh drag gesture not directly exercised due to the iOS 26.3 simulator tool limitation described above
823	- Code-reviewer verdict: issues fixed in this commit — critical finding on 4 stale test scripts with old-shape local `signMarketifyJwt` helpers (without fix, every role-gated test case would have started returning 401 after the refactor); one IMPORTANT finding on `docs/tech-architecture.md` §4.4 documenting the old JWT shape is handled as a Spec gap (see below) per the no-docs-edit rule.
824	- Spec gap: `docs/tech-architecture.md` §4.4 (~line 441) still shows the old 3-claim JWT shape (`role: "creator|lister"`) and the `current_user_role()` snippet at ~line 846 still reads `auth.jwt() ->> 'role'`. Code now supersedes those, but the spec remains authoritative per Ralph's hard rules — future iterations that re-derive JWT code from the spec alone would regenerate the bug. Flagged for a later spec-alignment story; do not regress.
825	- **Learnings for future iterations:**
826	  - PostgREST's `role` claim is the Postgres DB role, NOT the app role. Any custom-JWT edge-function stack that talks to PostgREST must either (a) put `"authenticated"` (or another real DB role) in `role` and keep the app role on a sibling claim like `app_role`, or (b) create a Postgres role for each app role name it wants to use (much harder to manage + auditors hate it). (a) is sanctioned by Supabase docs and is the standard.
827	  - The 401 PGRST301 error body "No suitable key or wrong key type" can be caused by role-claim resolution failures, NOT just secret mismatches. When investigating PGRST301, always verify the `role` claim resolves to a real Postgres role BEFORE re-rotating the JWT secret.
828	  - When refactoring shared claim shapes, grep `scripts/test/**` (and any other fixture/helper directories) for local re-implementations of the JWT signer — copy-pasted helpers are the top source of breakage in this repo because code-reviewer's primary diff scope is the staged files, not the whole repo.
829	  - iOS 26.3 simulator + mobile-mcp: upward swipes work, downward swipes trigger the system home gesture. For any future pull-to-refresh story, plan on using a backend probe script as the verification substitute and note the limitation in `notes` before the iteration stalls on trying to coerce the injected swipe.
830	- **Promoting to Codebase Patterns:** (below) JWT claim-structure rule (`role` vs `app_role`); the PGRST301 diagnostic heuristic; the "grep scripts/test for local JWT signers when refactoring claims" checkpoint; the iOS 26.3 mobile-mcp downward-swipe limitation.
831	
832	- **JWT claim structure for PostgREST-facing custom JWTs:** the top-level `role` claim is RESERVED by PostgREST — it names the Postgres DB role the connection switches to for the duration of the request (see PostgREST docs). Our tokens always set `role: "authenticated"` (the standard Supabase DB role). The app-level creator/lister distinction lives on a separate `app_role` claim, and all edge-function gate checks + RLS helpers read `app_role`. If you ever see `401 PGRST301 "No suitable key or wrong key type"` on a token that is otherwise correctly signed, the FIRST thing to check is whether `role` resolves to a real Postgres role — only after ruling that out should you investigate secret rotation. The error text is misleading.
833	- **When refactoring a shared JWT/claim helper, grep `scripts/test/**` before committing.** Four test scripts (`apply-to-listing.ts`, `get-listing-detail.ts`, `metrics-refresh.ts`, `submit-video.ts`) each carry a copy-pasted local `signMarketifyJwt` helper — they were written before the `_shared/jwt.ts` helper existed and deliberately don't import it (so they can mint "wrong-secret" and "wrong-role" tokens for negative test cases). Any change to the canonical claim shape must land in all 4 local copies in the same commit or every role-gated test case starts returning 401 silently. code-reviewer catches this if briefed on the full diff scope; don't rely on tsc because the local helpers are self-contained with their own claim types.
834	- **iOS 26.3 simulator intercepts mobile-mcp `mobile_swipe_on_screen` direction="down" as a system home gesture.** The app exits to the home screen before RN's gesture system registers the pan, regardless of geometry/distance/speed (tested 5+ variations at y=180/217/230/255/260/500, distances 60-200, widths 100-200 — all exit). Upward swipes work normally. For any pull-to-refresh story, plan on verifying the backend path via a probe script (`scripts/test/probe-*.ts` shape) as the substitute, and document the mobile-mcp blocker in the story `notes`. Do not block the story on a drag verification that the tool physically cannot produce on this simulator version.
835	---

For any pull-to-refresh story, plan on verifying the backend path via a probe script (`scripts/test/probe-*.ts` shape) as the substitute, and document the mobile-mcp blocker in the story `notes`. Do not block the story on a drag verification that the tool physically cannot produce on this simulator version.
- **Eligibility predicate form matters — use `(threshold is null or threshold <= metric)`, not `coalesce(threshold, 0) <= coalesce(metric, 0)`.** docs/tech-architecture.md §6.4 line 1872-1873 is the canonical form. Semantic difference: a NULL threshold means "no requirement" (short-circuits true), while `coalesce(threshold, 0)` conflates NULL with an explicit zero requirement (still evaluates the RHS). Index-friendliness also differs: the btree partial indexes on `min_followers_tiktok`/`min_followers_instagram` target the bare `<=` comparison, so the canonical form plans cleaner. Applies to every future RPC or migration that filters listings against creator cache columns. Ref: `supabase/migrations/20260418030100_us_038_discover_feed_null_threshold_semantics.sql` — a follow-up migration that fixed the original US-038 RPC after code-reviewer flagged the divergence.
- **`'` (and other HTML entities) render LITERALLY inside React Native `<Text>`.** RN's Text is not HTML — it has no entity parser. Web-style JSX like `<Text>here's</Text>` ships the 7 characters `h-e-r-e-&-a-p-o-s-;-s` to the screen. Use either (a) a JS string literal with a real Unicode char: `{'here\u2019s'}` / `{"here's"}`, or (b) an inline curly expression `{`here${'\u2019'}s`}`. Same rule applies to ` ` (use `\u00a0`), `—` (`—`), `&` (`&`). Be especially vigilant in apostrophe-containing copy that was copy-pasted from design docs or web prototypes where react's HTML entity escape is valid. Ref: `app/(creator)/feed.tsx` EmptyState body.
- **Sequence counter (monotonic ref + early-return guard) for async fetches triggered by fast-changing inputs.** Distinct from Pattern #106 (binary lock): a lock PREVENTS overlapping fetches; a sequence counter ALLOWS them but discards stale responses so the latest dispatch always wins. Pattern: `const seqRef = useRef(0); const load = useCallback(async () => { const seq = ++seqRef.current; const {data} = await rpc(...); if (seqRef.current !== seq) return; setRows(data); }, []);` — use when rapid toggle/filter/search-box changes each trigger their own fetch and you want the final response on-screen. Do NOT combine with a lock in the same handler — pick one discipline per callback. Ref: `loadSeqRef` in `app/(creator)/feed.tsx` US-038, guarding rapid "Eligible only" chip flips.
- **AsyncStorage is the sanctioned storage for non-secret preference booleans/strings** (filter toggles, last-selected tab, onboarding-seen flags). SecureStore is for credentials only (Pattern #91/#92 — JWT, refresh tokens); MMKV is unavailable under Expo Go (Pattern #91). Package `@react-native-async-storage/async-storage` is already in `package.json`. Pattern: store as `'true'`/`'false'` strings (not JSON.stringify — simpler to grep, survives corrupt reads gracefully), hydrate via a `useEffect` + `hydrated` gate identical to AuthProvider's pattern so the first render matches the default and the post-hydration render flips in place. Never gate UI on the presence of a storage key — `getItem` returning `null` just means "never set," which is the default state. Ref: `FILTER_STORAGE_KEY` in `app/(creator)/feed.tsx`.

## 2026-04-18 03:00 — US-038
- New screen `app/(creator)/feed.tsx` (~270 lines) replaces the US-040 era __DEV__ launcher placeholder with the real Discover feed: `SafeAreaView > ScrollView` with `RefreshControl` pull-to-refresh, "Discover" display header, a single-row filter chip ("Eligible only", default on), and a list of `CampaignCard`s sourced from a new SECURITY DEFINER RPC. Loading → three SkeletonCards. Error → inline copy. Empty → the docs/design.md §5.5 two-action empty state: "Refresh your metrics" (routes to Profile where US-036's refresh affordance lives) and "See all campaigns" (flips the toggle off in place; hidden when toggle is already off). Tapping a card routes to `/(creator)/listing/[id]`.
- New migration `supabase/migrations/20260418030000_us_038_list_discover_feed_rpc.sql` + follow-up `20260418030100_us_038_discover_feed_null_threshold_semantics.sql`. The function `public.list_discover_feed(p_eligible_only boolean)` returns a flat join of `listings + users (lister_handle)` under `l.status='active'`, with optional eligibility filtering against the caller's `creator_profiles` cache columns. DEFINER+owned-ship check bypasses the self-only `users_self_select` RLS (Pattern #117) that would have nulled lister_handle on every row under a PostgREST embed. Follow-up migration replaces the eligibility predicate with the canonical `(threshold is null or threshold <= creator_metric)` form (matches docs/tech-architecture.md §6.4 + btree partial indexes) — first migration kept for append-only history.
- Toggle persistence: AsyncStorage under `marketify.discoverEligibleOnly` (Pattern for non-secret preference storage added this iteration). Hydrate-then-flip gate copy of AuthProvider pattern (Pattern #97). Ref-mirror `eligibleRef` prevents stale capture in the `load` callback (Pattern #106). New this iteration: `loadSeqRef` sequence counter so rapid toggle flips discard stale responses (sibling pattern to #106, added below).
- Files changed:
  - `app/(creator)/feed.tsx` (from placeholder to full feed)
  - `supabase/migrations/20260418030000_us_038_list_discover_feed_rpc.sql` (new)
  - `supabase/migrations/20260418030100_us_038_discover_feed_null_threshold_semantics.sql` (new)
  - `src/types/supabase.ts` (regenerated — adds `list_discover_feed` function signature)
  - `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-038-eligible-on-empty.png` (new — empty state with both action cards + correctly-rendered curly apostrophe)
  - `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-038-eligible-off-list.png` (new — toggle off shows both Acme listings with $2,500 and $150 prices + @acme_studio handles, proving DEFINER RPC joins lister handles past self-only RLS)
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ (no new findings after the two US-038 migrations) / mobile-mcp ✓ (iPhone 17 Pro sim, both toggle states screenshotted)
- Code-reviewer verdict: issues fixed in this commit — 3 IMPORTANT findings all addressed: (1) original SQL eligibility predicate used `coalesce(threshold, 0)` form which diverges from canonical spec §6.4 and conflates null-threshold with explicit-zero → shipped a follow-up migration with the correct `threshold is null or threshold <= metric` form. (2) `'` HTML entity in the EmptyState body `<Text>` renders literally in React Native (not parsed) → replaced with `'here\u2019s'` JS string literal. (3) No stale-response guard on the `load` callback — rapid toggle flips could let an older RPC response overwrite a newer one → added `loadSeqRef` monotonic counter + early-return guard. All fixes re-verified via tsc/lint + mobile-mcp before commit.
- Spec gap: AC says `app/(creator)/discover.tsx` and MMKV storage; both diverge from project reality (`feed.tsx` matches the US-031 TabBar wiring + docs/design.md §3.1; AsyncStorage is the Expo Go-compatible substitute per Codebase Pattern #91). Same AC-vs-canonical divergence resolution as US-043.
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - Eligibility SQL form: always `(threshold is null or threshold <= metric)`, never `coalesce(threshold, 0)`. Affects every future RPC or migration that filters listings/offers against creator cache columns. Promoted below.
  - HTML entities like `'` render literally in RN `<Text>`. Promoted below.
  - Sequence counter pattern (distinct from Pattern #106 binary lock) for fast-changing fetch triggers. Promoted below.
  - AsyncStorage is the right storage for non-secret preference booleans — SecureStore is overkill, MMKV is Expo Go-incompatible. Promoted below.
  - When the story AC specifies a filename that conflicts with the existing TabBar wiring or canonical URL, resolve per docs/design.md (which this project treats as spec-authoritative) — same resolution precedent as US-043.
- **Promoting to Codebase Patterns:** (above) canonical eligibility predicate form; `'` renders literally in RN Text; sequence counter pattern for fast-changing fetches; AsyncStorage for non-secret preferences.

lines: - **JWT claim structure for PostgREST-facing custom JWTs:** the top-level `role` claim is RESERVED by PostgREST — it names the Postgres DB role the connection switches to for the duration of the request (see PostgREST docs). Our tokens always set `role: "authenticated"` (the standard Supabase DB role). The app-level creator/lister distinction lives on a separate `app_role` claim, and all edge-function gate checks + RLS helpers read `app_role`. If you ever see `401 PGRST301 "No suitable key or wrong key type"` on a token that is otherwise correctly signed, the FIRST thing to check is whether `role` resolves to a real Postgres role — only after ruling that out should you investigate secret rotation. The error text is misleading.

- **When refactoring a shared JWT/claim helper, grep `scripts/test/**` before committing.** Four test scripts (`apply-to-listing.ts`, `get-listing-detail.ts`, `metrics-refresh.ts`, `submit-video.ts`) each carry a copy-pasted local `signMarketifyJwt` helper — they were written before the `_shared/jwt.ts` helper existed and deliberately don't import it (so they can mint "wrong-secret" and "wrong-role" tokens for negative test cases). Any change to the canonical claim shape must land in all 4 local copies in the same commit or every role-gated test case starts returning 401 silently. code-reviewer catches this if briefed on the full diff scope; don't rely on tsc because the local helpers are self-contained with their own claim types.
